what_it_does: Defines ConceptV design-system CSS tokens for color, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, motion, layout primitives, semantic text classes, global scope defaults, tonal surface zones, palette variants, and zone helper classes.
observations: Imports Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts.
Uses a warm paper-first palette with off-cream surfaces, warm ink colors, gold and bronze accents, semantic status colors, and retained legacy aliases.
Defines radii, shadows, spacing, type families, tracking tokens, motion durations, easing, and layout width primitives as CSS custom properties.
Provides semantic typography utility classes such as .cv-display, .cv-h1, .cv-body, .cv-caption, .cv-eyebrow, .cv-mono, and .cv-screen-title.
Defines .cv-scope defaults for typography, background, headings, paragraphs, code, smoothing, and text rendering.
Implements a surface-zone registry using pigment variables, intensity knobs, and color-mix(in oklch, ...) derived roles for surface, strong, edge, and ink.
Includes cooler and warmer palette variants that recolor the system by overriding pigment variables.
Adds zone helper classes that apply background and border-color from zone tokens.
standouts: The file combines token definitions with detailed comments describing intended use, restraint, and semantic roles.
The zoning system derives many UI surface roles from pigment variables plus global intensity controls.
Gold is explicitly modeled as a Vi or active-decision voice rather than a default background treatment.
conventions: CSS custom properties are grouped into commented sections by surface, foreground, accent, status, border, shadow, radius, spacing, type, motion, layout, and zones.
Token naming is mostly role-based, with prefixes such as --paper, --ink, --accent, --status, --border, --shadow, --r, --s, --font, --tracking, --layout, --zone, --pig, and --vi.
Utility classes use .cv-* for typography and scope defaults, and .zone-* for tonal zone helpers.
Spacing comments state a strict 8px rhythm, while the scale includes intermediate 4px, 12px, and 20px values and duplicate aliases such as --s-7/--s-8 and --s-9/--s-10.
Several motion and intensity tokens carry inline /* @kind other */ annotations.
concerns: There is a malformed-looking section comment before the Vi tokens: it opens with /* and contains a trailing * instead of the same divider style used elsewhere.
The spacing scale names include duplicate values, which may make token choice ambiguous from this file alone.
The file imports external webfonts directly, so any surface importing it also triggers that network dependency.
notes: Brief someone on this as the ConceptV CSS token and utility layer for warm paper surfaces, typography, and semantic tonal zoning.
questions: Should the duplicate spacing values represent intentional aliases or historical compatibility names?
Are the inline @kind other annotations consumed by tooling or only notes?
Is the Vi section comment formatting intentional CSS comment style or a typo?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it acts as a source token layer for colors, type, spacing, layout, and reusable semantic styling utilities.
apparent_themes: warm ink instead of pure black
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to keep ConceptV visual surfaces synchronized through shared tokens while encoding design guidance about restraint, semantic color use, and low-opacity zoning directly beside the CSS values.
novelty: The pigment-plus-intensity zone registry is distinct: many semantic surfaces are generated from a small set of undertone variables and global tuning knobs.
contribution: Contributes a warm paper/color vocabulary, typography presets, layout and spacing primitives, motion tokens, semantic zone roles, palette-variant mechanism, and lightweight helper classes that a fused system would keep as reusable visual substrate definitions.
summary_for_embedding: CSS design-system file defining ConceptV color, type, spacing, shadow, radius, motion, layout, semantic typography, scoped defaults, and tonal zone tokens with helper classes.
intention_for_embedding: Created to provide a shared visual substrate for ConceptV surfaces so deck, UI kit, and mock interfaces can use synchronized tokens and semantic zoning.
